audit_items.proof_trace: All allocations in sass_make_error are correctly managed on every reachable path: if calloc fails, v is never allocated and NULL is returned; if msg is NULL, v is allocated then immediately freed via the guard at L176 before returning NULL; on success, both v and v->error.message are returned to the caller and freed by sass_delete_value (L221-223, L230). The guard at L176 that appears to handle sass_copy_c_string failure is dead code in practice because sass_alloc_memory (sass.cpp:40-43) calls exit(EXIT_FAILURE) on OOM rather than returning NULL, so sass_copy_c_string can never return NULL.
